title: Three northern districts on yellow alert on Saturday - The Hindu
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Haryana
full_text:
Isolated heavy rainfall is likely to continue over a few districts in Kerala over the weekend, an India Meteorological Department (IMD) update on the southwest monsoon rainfall indicated on Friday (June 12, 2026).
Kozhikode, Kannur, and Kasaragod are on yellow alert for isolated heavy rainfall on Saturday (June 13, 2026). Pathanamthitta, Kottayam, Ernakulam, Idukki, and Thrissur are on yellow alert on Sunday (June 14, 2026). All other districts can expect light to moderate rainfall on the two days.
Weather systems, including an upper air cyclonic circulation over west-central Bay of Bengal and adjoining coastal Andhra Pradesh, a trough extending from east-central Arabian Sea to west-central Bay of Bengal, and an upper air cyclonic circulation over north interior Karnataka are influencing the current spell.
As per the latest IMD updates, Kerala has so far recorded 22% excess rainfall this southwest monsoon season (between June 1 and June 12). So far, seven districts have recorded excess rainfall while the other seven districts have reported normal rainfall for the period.
Published - June 12, 2026 08:57 pm IST
